items.title: GPT-5.5-Cyber built a zlib fuzzing lab in a day
items.summary: We’re running Patch the Planet, an ongoing collaboration with OpenAI that pairs Trail of Bits engineers directly with more than 30 open-source projects. Its goal is to front-run a serious problem facing open-source maintainers: highly capable models like GPT-5.5-Cyber will soon c

items.title: Bitcoin’s BIP 110 fork deadline nears with miner support at zero
items.summary: The BIP 110 proposal would cap arbitrary data on Bitcoin for a year, but Saylor, Adam Back and others say turning a spam dispute into a consensus fight could create a bigger risk than the spam itself.

items.title: AI found an Ethereum bug that could take validators offline, but humans had to prove it
items.summary: The Ethereum Foundation pointed coordinated AI agents at the software its validators run and got a remotely triggerable crash out of it. It also got a pile of confident, well-written findings that were not bugs at all.

items.title: Mutation testing comes to DAML
items.summary: In April we released Mewt, our open-source mutation-testing engine that finds the gaps in your test suite. Today we’re expanding it with support for DAML, the language Canton Network applications are written in. Mewt now reads DAML, generates several classes of mutants (including

items.title: Bitcoin, ether little changed as U.S. launches fresh Iran strikes
items.summary: The U.S. hit Iran for the third time this week and Tehran has reportedly closed the Strait of Hormuz again.

items.title: Ripple once weighed shutting down and handing XRP to shareholders, CEO says
items.summary: Ripple's Brad Garlinghouse says he and co-founder Chris Larsen considered winding the company down and handing its XRP to shareholders before deciding to fight the 2020 lawsuit.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Bitcoin bulls Michael Saylor, Adam Back slam BIP-110 Ordinals proposal
items.summary: The ongoing debate comes despite a broad downturn in Ordinals transaction activity over the last two years.

items.title: Empery Digital shares rise after selling Bitcoin to fund AI data center project
items.summary: The sales come months after a major Empery shareholder demanded the firm ditch its Bitcoin treasury strategy and force the resignation of its CEO and board.

items.title: Lending protocol Bonzo loses 77% of value locked as $9 million oracle exploit rattles Hedera
items.summary: Bonzo Lend lost approximately $9.05 million after an attacker exploited a verification flaw in a third-party Supra oracle contract on the Hedera network.

items.title: Crypto IPO market stalls as capital rotates to AI and macro uncertainty weighs
items.summary: Funding constraints and investor caution, not regulation, are delaying crypto IPOs, according to Cohen & Company Capital Markets' Christian Lopez.

items.title: The UK has finally shown it’s serious about crypto
items.summary: Several recent regulatory steps indicate the UK might finally stop dragging its feet when it comes to crypto, argues Wirex CEO Chet Shah.

items.title: Bitcoin treasury company Empery Digital sold about half of its BTC stack
items.summary: It's a sign of the times as the troubled company swaps its bitcoin treasury ambitions for AI data centers.

items.title: Bitcoin analysts predict $300,000–$500,000 price in 2029. The math says no
items.summary: Analysts predict a rally to $300,000 or more by 2029. But key data suggests the era of moonshots may be over.

items.title: Bitcoin nearing late stages of bear market: Jamie Coutts, Real Vision
items.summary: Real Vision chief crypto analyst Jamie Coutts said it's far too early to call $1 million by 2030, but he's confident Bitcoin could climb up to $250,000 over the next couple of years.

items.title: Ethereum climbs 3% on tokenization boom: Can bulls push ETH price past $1,800?
items.summary: Despite strong tokenization gains and institutional accumulation, weak onchain and derivatives data leaves ETH vulnerable to a $1,700 retest.

items.title: Bonzo Lend loses $9M in oracle exploit on Hedera
items.summary: An attacker inflated the value of SAUCE collateral and borrowed $9 million from Bonzo Lend through a flaw in Supra’s onchain oracle verifier.

items.title: Bitcoin price gains nearly 10% in July, but traders still see BTC copying 2022 bear market
items.summary: Bitcoin price gains approached 10% for the first two weeks of the month, but analysis warned of the bear market returning from August onward.

items.title: Dollar stablecoins could improve FX access but amplify currency runs: IMF paper
items.summary: An IMF working paper says dollar stablecoins can improve access to foreign currency but may also help coordinate exits from local currencies during periods of severe exchange-rate stress.

items.title: Robinhood says its AI agent feature will ‘soon’ be assisting crypto traders
items.summary: More than 70,000 agentic accounts have already been created by Robinhood equities and options traders since late May, when Robinhood launched a beta version of the product.
